pi_items.source: task.json description (line 5), export_result.sh (line 48-49 confirms registry.models.Aircraft), standard Django admin URL pattern for Aircraft model in registry app

privileged_info_summary: The register_aircraft task uses Aerobridge, an open-source drone fleet management system (https://github.com/openskies-sh/aerobridge). The task requires adding a synthetic test aircraft named 'Phoenix Mk3' with flight controller ID 'PHXFC2024001' to the local Django admin interface at http://localhost:8000/admin/registry/aircraft/add/. Authentication uses admin username 'admin' and password 'adminpass123'. The Aircraft model includes fields for name, operator, manufacturer, flight_controller_id, status, and final_assembly. The setup_task.sh script removes any pre-existing Phoenix Mk3 aircraft and creates exactly one complete aircraft assembly (status=2) for the task. The export_result.sh script verifies the Aircraft record was created by querying the registry.models.Aircraft model. The verifier checks that the aircraft count increased and the aircraft name matches 'Phoenix Mk3' exactly.
